name: Bhai Lal Ji (Nankana Sahib)
body: As most would know, I have been uploading recordings onto my Youtube channel ‘Kirtan Sewa’ for many years now. To date, some of the most popular uploads have been of Bhai Lal Ji. His Guldasta ‘Dekhan Kou Mustak’ and the Shabad ‘Gobind Milan Ki Eh Teri Bhariya’ are the most viewed videos of my channel. Bhai Lal Ji was born in 1929 as Ashiq Ali in Goindwal Sahib. His father was a Hazuri Raagi in Goindwal Sahib, from whom he started his initial training. Then, he was sent to Amritsar in 1940, where he learned from Bhai Ghasita for 2 years. Subsequently, he began his apprenticeship with the illustrious Bhai Chand. He has stated it was during this time he began to be known as Bhai Lal and it has stuck since. Its also worth to note that there was a very well known rababi in Sri Harmandir Sahib by the name of Bhai Lal Mohamad Amritsari.  He was much senior to Ashiq Ali, and in the later years Bhai Lal Mohamad had taken a break from singing due to the death of his eldest son. It has been speculated that he  adopted the name Bhai Lal due to the popularity of Bhai Lal Mohamad. Anyway, Bhai Lal Ji left for Pakistan during the partition, just like many other rababis who feared for their lives during that period of turmoil. He settled in Lahore and got married. He has four sons and currently they have formed a jatha and are following in his footsteps. Bhai Lal Ji passed away a few years ago at the age of 85. If anyone has more recordings to share of Bhai Lal Ji, please contact us.

The next batch of recordings were recorded in Leeds (UK) and contributed by Dr. Manjit Singh Hunjin, who is a very talented artist. Please check out his Instagram page at the following link https://www.instagram.com/amsimeetart/?hl=en
